data.title: U.S. consumer credit falls in February
data.description:
U.S. consumer credit fell by $19.9B month-over-month in February, compared to a $6.0B sequential drop logged in January, according to data released by the Federal Reserve on Friday.

Developing... Check back for updates.

data.title: National Fuel Gas initiated Overweight at KeyBanc as well-positioned integrated gas firm
data.description:
National Fuel Gas (NFG) up 1.5% in Tuesday's trading as KeyBanc initiated coverage with an Overweight rating and $110 price target, saying the company remains an under-the-radar energy story despite ongoing growth initiatives and positive catalysts playing out.

National Fuel Gas' (NFG) pending $2.6B acquisition of CenterPoint Energy’s Ohio gas utility business creates a much larger local distribution company and doubles the rate base, and the increased scale for this regulated business further stabilizes the broader earnings profile while also fortifying its investment-grade rating, KeyBanc's Tim Rezvan said.

In the company's Integrated Upstream & Gathering segment, Rezvan sees efficiency tailwinds from the recent activity pivot to the more prolific Eastern Development Area, where the Utica shale interval is proving to be highly productive and consistent; delineation efforts continue to improve the company's inventory depth, estimated at 15 years of sub-$1.75/mcf inventory at the current pace of 25-27 wells/year.

Given current limited local demand growth, the analyst sees challenges to significantly ramping production but noted that National Fuel Gas (NFG) grew volumes at a 7% compound annual growth rate during FY 2023-25, with capex declining at a 5% compound annual growth rate over the same period, results that speak to the improving productivity of the Tioga County Utica, the focus of NFG's drilling program.

Looking to the regulated segments, Rezvan said he sees organic and inorganic growth opportunities driving scale and a stronger and more stable earnings base; the Utility segment is progressing the $2.6B acquisition of CNP Ohio, a transformational deal that doubles the rate base and expands the LDC footprint from legacy utility operating areas in New York and Pennsylvania.

data.title: Blue Line Capital’s Bill Baruch finds “great value” in beaten-down software stocks
data.description:
Bill Baruch, head of Blue Line Capital, sees significant opportunity in the software sector (IGV), (IGPT), (XSW) after recent market turbulence, identifying names like ServiceNow (NOW), Oracle (ORCL), and Microsoft (MSFT) as attractive investments at current levels.

In an interview with CNBC, Baruch expressed a bullish outlook on the broader market while highlighting tech stocks (VGT), (XLK), (IYW) that have been unfairly punished during the recent selloff.

The market sentiment shifted dramatically after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated the central bank would not raise rates in response to oil shocks, according to Baruch.

He noted that “pessimism was peaking” when the VIX (VIX) tested 30, but the market’s “heartbeat” has since turned more positive.

“Monday night looked ugly, and things turned,” Baruch said, adding that the market has shown resilience despite negative headlines.

From a technical perspective, Baruch is watching the 6,600 level in the S&P 500 (SP500), (SPX) as a critical support zone. He believes that holding above this level opens a path toward 6,800, near the 200-day moving average.

The strategist suggested the market is “one good piece of news away” from a 300-handle rally in the S&P 500 (SP500), potentially coupled with a $10 drop in oil prices.

Baruch pointed to a “bifurcation” in the market where software stocks (XSW), (IGV) have been “bludgeoned” despite their underlying value.

“There are some really great names that have been beaten down in the software space,” he said, noting that he is watching for new points of leadership as the market begins to rally.

He also mentioned Micron (MU) in the memory space as another name that has been “smacked down from its highs.”

His top picks in the software sector include ServiceNow (NOW), Oracle (ORCL), and Microsoft (MSFT), which he called “great values” at current prices. These names represent areas where Blue Line Capital sees the strongest opportunity for outperformance.

Blue Line Capital recently deployed half of its 8% cash reserve to purchase software stocks before the latest market shift.

Baruch explained that in typical geopolitical situations, he prefers to maintain cash for buying opportunities, but the value in beaten-down software was too compelling to ignore.

If oil prices (CO1:COM), (CL1:COM) decline and positive headlines emerge, he believes it “could be a really interesting month of April and May,” with momentum potentially carrying into June.

data.title: Wayfair plans to open its first store in Florida
data.description:
Wayfair Inc. (W) plans to continue to grow its retail store footprint with a large-format store in Florida at Galleria Fort Lauderdale. The 94,000-square-foot store is scheduled to open in late 2027.

Like its stores in the Chicago area and Atlanta, Wayfair's (W) Fort Lauderdale location is being designed to reflect the local community, with assortments tailored to the South Florida customer and lifestyle. The store, which will be the company's first in Florida, will feature products and design inspiration that align with the region's coastal aesthetic, indoor-outdoor living, and year-round entertaining.

"As we continue to grow our retail footprint, entering Florida with a namesake store is an exciting next step for Wayfair," highlighted Wayfair (W) Vice President of Merchandising and Stores Liza Lefkowski. "Galleria Fort Lauderdale's transformation creates an ideal setting for us to introduce our in-store experience to a new community of customers," she added.

Many items at the Fort Lauderdale store will be available for immediate take-home, while larger pieces such as sofas, dining sets, and outdoor furniture can be delivered fast and free through Wayfair's best-in-class logistics network. Fort Lauderdale is seen by Wayfair (W) as representing an ideal market for the brand's continued expansion into high-impact regions across the U.S.

The Fort Lauderdale store announcement marks Wayfair's (W) sixth store announcement. The locations in Wilmette, Illinois, and Atlanta, Georgia, are open, while the locations in Columbus, Ohio, and Denver, Colorado, are opening later this year, and the Westchester, New York, location will open in early 2027.

data.title: Bayer U.S. chief says tariffs won’t impact 2026 outlook
data.description:
The head of U.S. operations at Bayer (BAYZF) told Reuters on Tuesday that the German drugmaker doesn’t see a requirement to revise its 2026 estimates to account for the Trump administration’s recently imposed tariffs on pharmaceutical imports.

Last week, President Donald Trump signed an executive order introducing new tariffs on pharma and biotech firms that have not reached "most favored nation" drug pricing deals with the White House.

The levies expected to take effect for most companies in September could impact Bayer (BAYRY), as the German drugmaker is not among the 16 pharma companies that have already entered into such agreements with the U.S.

"We feel that we've appropriately anticipated tariffs as we think about our 2026 guidance," said Sebastian Guth, who serves as head of Bayer's (BAYRY) U.S. business and the chief operating officer of its pharma division

In March, Bayer (BAYZF) estimated €9.1B - €9.6B ($10.6B - $11.2B) of adjusted EBITDA for 2026, falling short of the €9.67B projected by analysts.

He added that the company is particularly at ease with its outlook because the U.S. is committed to its 2025 trade deal with the EU that sets a 15% baseline tariff on nearly all imports from the bloc.

Guth also referred to a recently finalized U.S.-UK pharmaceutical trade deal under which Britain agreed to boost its drug spending to 0.6% from 0.3% of GDP over the years in exchange for tariff-free access to the U.S.

He identified the deal as ​a basis on which rich countries can make decisions about changing their pricing structures. "There's an acknowledgment that it isn't going to happen overnight but will happen over time," Guth said.

data.title: U.S. regulator approves rules that implement certain GENIUS Act requirements
data.description:
The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation board has approved a notice of proposed rulemaking, which is expected to implement certain requirements and standards under the GENIUS Act.

The Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act was passed last year, and the latest version of the Clarity Act intends to flesh out the GENIUS Act.

There were media reports about the possibility of the Clarity Act banning yield payments on passive stablecoin holdings, which impacted the stocks of Circle Internet (CRCL) and Coinbase Global (COIN).

In March, the Kalshi prediction market had forecasted an ~64% chance of the Clarity Act clearing before August this year.

The proposed set of rules, which the FDIC currently approved, is expected to establish a prudential framework for the FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers, laying out guidelines on requirements related to reserve assets, redemption, capital, and risk management standards.

The proposed rules are also expected to clarify requirements related to custodial and safekeeping services, and whether eligible tokenized deposits can be treated as any other types of deposits.

data.title: Wall Street sinks as ceasefire hopes diminish; U.S. hits military targets on Kharg Island
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were in the red on Tuesday as reports surfaced that the U.S. has attacked military targets on Kharg Island, while President Donald Trump threatened Iran's “imminent destruction” if the country does not agree to a ceasefire.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.4%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.6%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.5%.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.34%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 2 basis points to 3.84%.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were higher at $113, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $109 per barrel.

“A whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again. I don’t want that to happen, but it probably will,” Trump said on Truth Social, ahead of the 8 p.m. deadline he set for the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

“We will find out tonight, one of the most important moments in the long and complex history of the world. 47 years of extortion, corruption, and death will finally end,” he wrote.

Iran has allegedly responded by cutting off communication with the U.S.

In addition, the U.S. has reportedly attacked military targets on Kharg Island as the 8 p.m. ET deadline arrives. According to media reports, electricity supply was disrupted in parts of the Iranian island after projectiles struck transmission lines.

In economic news, durable goods orders fell more than expected in February.

Consumers' inflation expectations increased short- and medium-term, and held unchanged at the longer-term outlook, as gas price growth expectations jumped to the highest level since March 2022, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

The consumer credit data is due during market hours.

data.title: KKR in talks to sell BMC Helix to Montagu for $900M - report
data.description:
KKR & Co. (KKR) is in exclusive discussions to sell information technology firm BMC Helix to European private equity firm Montagu.

KKR and Montagu are working on a transaction that would value BMC Helix at ~$900 million, according to a Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Montagu is also in discussions with possible financing sources for the deal.

A deal could be announced within weeks, according to the report. A final agreement hasn't been reached, and talks could still fall apart.  

BMC Helix, KKR, and Montagu declined to comment to Bloomberg.

Bloomberg reported in February 2025 that KKR (KKR) was preparing to sell BMC Helix.

data.title: Weekly ETF flows: Five of 11 sectors record outflows; energy sector leads inflows
data.description:
The world's largest exchange-traded fund, SPDR S&P 500 Trust (SPY), saw outflows of $4.93B for the week ending April 3, while its price increased by 3.78%.

iShares Silver Trust ETF (SLV) recorded outflows totaling $302.59M, while its price increased by 3.57% during the week. On the other hand, the Gold SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD) recorded inflows totaling $220.23M last week while GLD prices rose 3.58% during the week.

iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) saw an outflow of $182.10M last week, while Bitcoin (BTC-USD) price slipped 1.36% during the same period.

Last week’s inflows/outflows

The 11 S&P 500 sector tracking ETFs collectively recorded inflows of about $106.57M last week, according to data from etfdb.com.

The top sector inflows were led by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE), which recorded $470.24M, followed by the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) with $268.97M and the Materials Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLB) at $262.06M.

On the other hand, the largest outflows were seen in the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY), totaling $583.03M, followed by the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP) with $248.83M in outflows, and the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC), which recorded $171.22M in outflows. Five of the 11 sectors recorded outflows in the last week.

data.title: Massive fire at Kimberly-Clark California warehouse weighs on shares
data.description:
A massive fire at a Kimberly-Clark (KBM) Southern California paper goods warehouse is pressuring shares on Tuesday as the blaze was seen engulfing nearly the entire 1.2 million square foot facility.

According to the deputy chief of the Ontario, California, fire department, Mike Wedell, the flames grew “exponentially,” resulting in the roof collapsing and forcing firefighters to retreat from the fire.

Resources from neighboring agencies were enlisted to battle the fire, with more than 140 personnel assisting the Ontario fire department. 

Wedell told media sources on site that it appears the six-alarm fire was set intentionally by an employee of the warehouse. A person of interest is currently in custody.

“Safety is our top priority, and we can confirm there are no reported injuries. The facility is operated by a third-party partner, and we are working closely with them and local authorities. We will share additional information as it becomes available,” Kimberly-Clark (KMB) said in a statement.

Shares are down 4% to a record low of $92.82.
